stories.title: Monday briefing: Hungary chooses Péter Magyar over Viktor Orbán
stories.summary: In today’s newsletter: Orbán concedes defeat after 16 years in power, ushering in a new era for Hungary’s relations with the EU, US and Russia Good morning. The people of Hungary are waking up in an unfamiliar political landscape – one in which Viktor Orbán, who has served as prime minister since 2010, is stepping aside after defeat to Péter Magyar, whose Tisza party has won an election likely to reshape the country’s ties with the EU, the US and Russia. Less than three hours after polls closed

stories.title: How Orbán lost the Hungarian election
stories.summary: How Orbán lost the Hungarian election For two years, the Hungarian leader watched his support slip away. By MAX GRIERA in Budapest Illustration by Natália Delgado/ POLITICO Viktor Orbán likely knew what was coming. When U.S. Vice President JD Vance stood beside him last week and predicted the Hungarian prime minister would win reelection Sunday, Orbán gave a small wave of his hand as if to say, “I’m not so sure.” Ever since his challenger Péter Magyar burst onto the scene in early 2024, the Hung

stories.title: Trump says US will blockade strait of Hormuz after Iran peace talks fail | First Thing
stories.summary: Tehran said the move would be a breach of the ceasefire. Plus, Viktor Orbán ousted in Hungarian election in blow to global far right Don’t already get First Thing in your inbox? Sign up here Good morning. Donald Trump has said the US will begin blockading the strait of Hormuz in an attempt to wrest control of the vital waterway from Iran after peace negotiations between the countries failed. When would the blockade start? US Central Command (Centcom) announced the blockade would begin on Monday

stories.title: Hormuz, Hungary and the UK shifting closer to the EU – podcast
stories.summary: After 16 years in power in Hungary, Viktor Orbán has been defeated, marking a huge shift in the European Union. Meanwhile, Keir Starmer has defended potential legislation that will align the UK with European rules – without a vote in parliament. Plus the government confirms the UK will not support Donald Trump’s planned blockade of the strait of Hormuz, but what will it offer instead? Guardian Live: Can Labour come back from the brink? With a difficult set of May elections approaching, Labour un

stories.title: EU leaders cheer Orbán’s defeat
stories.summary: Politicians across Europe celebrated Hungary’s Prime Minister-elect Péter Magyar after his conservative Tisza party won Sunday’s parliamentary elections, ousting long-time leader Viktor Orbán. Polish Prime Minister Donald Tusk  posted a video  of himself on the phone with the winner in Budapest , telling him “I think I am happier than you!” He captioned the video with the words “Welcome back to Europe!” alongside the Polish and Hungarian flags. German Chancellor

stories.title: Merz’s coalition agrees on joint measures to tackle Iran war fallout
stories.summary: BERLIN — The leaders of Germany’s centrist coalition on Monday announced a range of measures to tackle the fallout of the U.S.-Israeli war with Iran. Ministers in German Chancellor Friedrich Merz’s conservative alliance and their coalition partner, the center-left Social Democratic Party (SPD), had been at odds in recent weeks over the best way to address the growing strain that the war in the Middle East is placing on Germany’s already struggling economy. Monday’s measures wer

stories.title: ‘Hungary has chosen Europe’: EU leaders jubilant after Péter Magyar’s victory over Orbán
stories.summary: Congratulations pour in from across EU, with leaders from Spain, Poland, France, Britain, Denmark, Romania, Sweden and beyond hailing a new chapter Full report: Hungarian opposition ousts Viktor Orbán after 16 years in power EU leaders heaped praise on Péter Magyar after his decisive election victory in Hungary against the long-serving prime minister Viktor Orbán, who many saw as a direct threat to Europe’s peace and prosperity. The outpouring reflected a deep frustration with Orbán across the E

stories.title: What does Péter Magyar’s win in Hungary mean for the EU and Ukraine?
stories.summary: Tisza leader has promised to repair Budapest’s ties with Brussels after years of strained relations under Viktor Orbán Europe live – latest updates Péter Magyar’s resounding election victory brings an end to 16 years of rightwing populist rule under Viktor Orbán. But the effects of that win are likely to be felt well beyond Hungary’s borders, from Brussels to Moscow and from Kyiv to Washington. Continue reading...

stories.title: Hungarian election winner Magyar vows to rebuild EU relationship after stunning defeat of Viktor Orbán - Europe live
stories.summary: The Tisza leader said the electorate voted ‘not just for a change of government but for a change of the regime’ in Brussels The EU will start work with the new Hungarian government “as soon as possible” to make progress on issues including energy and the release of frozen European funds, the head of the European Commission has said. “We will start working with the government as soon as possible on the topics you mentioned and much more to make a swift and overdue progress to the benefit of the H

stories.title: Poland and South Korea upgrade ties to strategic defence partnership deal
stories.summary: During a visit to Seoul on Monday, Polish Prime Minister Donald Tusk and South Korean President Lee Jae-myung pledged to strengthen their cooperation, particularly in defence.

stories.title: Von der Leyen uses Orbán defeat to push for end of veto in EU foreign policy
stories.summary: BRUSSELS — European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen waited less than a day after Hungary voted Viktor Orbán out of office to call for the EU to get more power over national governments to force through foreign policy decisions. Governments should be able to push through EU policies ― on issues that could include sanctions on Russia and funds for Ukraine ― by majority rather than allowing individual nations to wield a veto, she told reporters in Brussels. Under Orbán, Hungary’s pr

stories.title: Hungarian opposition ousts Viktor Orbán after 16 years in power
stories.summary: Péter Magyar’s Tisza party wins election as prime minister concedes defeat, in result likely to reshape ties with EU Magyar set to outline Hungary plans –Europe live ‘Hungary has chosen Europe’: EU leaders jubilant after Magyar’s victory Hungary’s opposition Tisza party, led by Péter Magyar, has won the election, bringing an end to Viktor Orbán’s 16-year grip on power, in a result that is likely to rattle the White House and reshape the country’s relationship with the EU. Less than three hours a

stories.title: Viktor Orbán is gone. What does his fall mean for Europe? Our panel responds
stories.summary: Hungary’s return to democracy will be hard. But the impact of Péter Magyar’s decisive victory could be profound, inside the country and beyond Continue reading...

stories.title: Tony Abbott labels Viktor Orbán ‘Trump with brains’ as future of Budapest thinktank linked to former PM in doubt
stories.summary: Former Australian PM, who has been associated with pro-Orbán Danube Institute since 2023, praises ousted Hungarian leader Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Tony Abbott has likened Viktor Orbán to “[Donald] Trump with brains” and labelled him Hungary’s “greatest modern leader”, as the future of his work for the ousted leader’s pet thinktank hangs in the balance. Orbán’s 16-year grip on power in Hungary has ended after the rightwing populist leader conceded his Fidesz pa

stories.title: Hungary: Peter Magyar's election heralds thaw in EU ties
stories.summary: Following the ouster of Russia-friendly Viktor Orban after 16 years, Europe is now waiting to see if his designated successor Peter Magyar can make good on his promises to repair ties with Brussels.

stories.title: Viktor Orbán's Hungarian experiment runs out of steam
stories.summary: Péter Magyar's historic win offers peace for a country exhausted by the tensions of Viktor Orbán's rule, Nick Thorpe reports from Budapest.

stories.title: Hungary: jubilant scenes across Budapest follow Magyar’s victory
stories.summary: Hungary saw large crowds take to the streets on Sunday, 12 April 2026, after Prime Minister Viktor Orbán conceded defeat, ending 16 years in power.

stories.title: Katya Adler: Jubilation in Budapest will be felt in Europe but leaves Moscow cold
stories.summary: European leaders share Hungarians' joy over the ousting of Vladimir Putin's EU ally, writes the BBC's Europe editor.

stories.title: Orbán era swept away by Péter Magyar's Hungary election landslide
stories.summary: Viktor Orbán's 16-year rule is over, defeated by a 45-year-old ex-party insider who convinced a majority of Hungarians to oust him.

stories.title: Hungary election: Supporters of Peter Magyar's party celebrate in Budapest
stories.summary: Supporters of Peter Magyar, leader of the pro-European conservative TISZA party, celebrate on the banks on the river Danube in Budapest.

stories.title: Orbán loses, Magyar wins, Europe reacts — live updates
stories.summary: Viktor Orbán was ousted as prime minister of Hungary after Péter Magyar won a stunning victory in the national election on Sunday. Longtime leader Orbán, Europe’s most prominent populist-nationalist politician, had frustrated Brussels’ agenda on multiple files, including a €90B loan to help Ukraine in its fight against Russia. After 16 years in power, Orbán’s exit is set to change EU dynamics. Follow all the reaction below to Orbán’s loss, as Brussels gears up for the new

stories.title: Seismic shift: How 16 years of Hungarian politics changed in one day
stories.summary: The National Election Commission said voter turnout was at a record 77.8%, the highest ever recorded in a Hungarian election.

stories.title: Orbán and Magyar vote in high-stakes Hungarian election watched by EU
stories.summary: Viktor Orbán and Péter Magyar vote in a pivotal Hungarian election with record turnout expected, as the result could reshape Budapest’s role in the EU.

stories.title: 'A truly historic moment': Hungarian opposition wins election landslide
stories.summary: Rajini Vaidyanathan broadcasts from outside Hungary's parliament as crowds hear about the prime minister's concession.

stories.title: A Mi Hazánk nem lett a mérleg nyelve, de több szavazatot szerzett, mint négy éve
stories.summary: A Mi Hazánk valószínűleg újra hat mandátumhoz jut, arányaiban szinte ugyanannyi szavazója volt, mint 2022-ben, de összességében bő nyolcezer fővel bővült a tábora.

stories.title: Nulla pénzből is folytatja a Magyar Kétfarkú Kutya Párt
stories.summary: „Ne utáljátok azt, aki hatalomra került. Kritizálni lehet” – mondta a választási eredményekről Nagy Dávid, a Kutyapárt listavezetője a szimpatizánsoknak a párt eredményváróján.

stories.title: „A győzelmünk nem a Holdról, hanem minden magyar ablakból látszik”
stories.summary: „A kilencvenes választás sem volt ekkora eufória” – mondta egy Tisza-szavazó, miután pártja tizenhat év után legyőzte a Fideszt. A Tisza legboldogabb politikusa egyértelműen Hegedűs Zsolt, a párt egészségügyi szakértője volt.

stories.title: Felrobbant Budapest a Tisza győzelme után
stories.summary: 16 év után másfél óra alatt vége lett Orbán Viktor kormányzásának. Feszültség, örömkönnyek és fesztelen tombolás a választás éjszakáján a fővárosban.

stories.title: The day the Tisza Party swept away Viktor Orbán’s 16-year regime
stories.summary: On April 12, 2026, Hungary’s 10th free election resulted in a landslide victory for the opposition. Through some of the best shots of our photographers, here’s a look at how the day unfolded for the two contending parties and how Budapest celebrated Péter Magyar’s triumph.

stories.title: Bódi Mátyás: Harmadszorra riogatta háborúval a Fidesz a választókat, ez mostanra fulladt ki
stories.summary: A Választási Földrajz szakértője szerint ami 2022-ben bejött, az 2026-ban már nem volt elég. A Fidesz mozgósítása nem volt rossz, de nem volt elég, Magyar Péter pedig népszavazás szintjére emelte a kampányt arról, hogy a Kelethez vagy a Nyugathoz akarunk-e tartozni.

stories.title: Ukraine no longer advises its citizens to avoid Hungary
stories.summary: The Ukrainian Foreign Ministry said that the Hungarian people's decision has demonstrated that "our neighbours desire to live n a truly independent Hungary that is part of a united and free Europe".

stories.title: Erős mínuszban indult a kormányközeli tőzsdei cégeknek a választás utáni reggel
stories.summary: Kétharmados győzelmet ért el a vasárnapi országgyűlési választáson a Tisza Párt, nagy mozgásokkal reagált a magyar tőzsde.

stories.title: A pilóták sztrájkja miatt sorra törli a budapesti járatait a Lufthansa
stories.summary: A pilóták kétnapos munkabeszüntetése miatt a keddi járatokat is törölték, a sztrájk kedd éjfélig tart.

stories.title: 40 éves az SOS Gyermekfalvak: a családot kell megerősíteni (x)
stories.summary: Miközben a magyar gyermekvédelem egyre súlyosabb válságban van, idén 40 éves az SOS Gyermekfalvak. A szervezet szerint a megoldás nem az intézmények bővítése, hanem a családok megerősítése – és már vannak működő példáik. Szilvási Lénával, a szervezet szakmai vezetőjével beszélgettünk.

stories.title: Ferencz Orsolya miniszteri biztos: A felelősöket ki kell vágnunk végre a nemzeti oldal testéből
stories.summary: A Fidesz-KDNP vereségének okaként a megmagyarázhatatlan mesés gazdagodást, a nepotizmust, a megélhetési karrierjobboldaliak falkáit nevezte meg.

stories.title: Hope devoured fear
stories.summary: In just two years, Péter Magyar convinced Hungarians that they have nothing to fear and that Viktor Orbán’s system can be replaced even within the framework created by Fidesz.

stories.title: „Make Tusványos Great Again” – a román sajtó szinte csak Magyar Péterről és Orbán Viktorról szól
stories.summary: Több román portál élőben követte a fejleményeket vasárnap, de elemzőket is megszólaltattak és véleménycikkekből sincs hiány. Az RMDSZ a magyar-magyar kapcsolatok újragondolásáról beszélt.

stories.title: Rendszerváltó házibulivá változott Budapest belvárosa a Fidesz bukása után
stories.summary: A Tisza Párt eredményváróján is óriási volt a buli, de a legváratlanabb utcabál a Wesselényi utcánál zajlott, ahol az autók alig tudtak haladni. Ez nem nagyon zavarta őket: dudáltak, pacsiztak a bulizókkal, még a rendőrök is mosolyogva hajtottak el közöttük.

stories.title: Ez volt Trump reakciója Orbán választási vereségére
stories.summary: Az amerikai elnöknek a Fehér Ház udvarán szegezték neki a kérdést.

stories.title: Budapesten koncertezik Cate le Bon, aki producerként is nagyon emlékezetes lemezeken dolgozott
stories.summary: A walesi előadó a Magyar Zene Házában lép fel augusztusban.

stories.title: Szőlő utcai ügy: meghosszabbította Kovács-Buna Károly és még három gyanúsított letartóztatását a bíróság
stories.summary: A volt igazgató mellett börtönben marad egy volt nevelő, gyermekfelügyelő, és az egyik rendész is.

stories.title: A legszegényebb településeken most is a Fidesz tarolt
stories.summary: Kicsit kisebb lett a Fidesz-fölény, mint 2022-ben, de így is akadt olyan község, ahol 98 százalék felett teljesítettek a kormánypártok.

stories.title: A Kreml reméli, hogy folytatódik a „rendkívül pragmatikus” kapcsolat Magyarország és Oroszország között
stories.summary: „Magyarország döntött, és mi ezt a döntést tiszteletben tartjuk”, mondta Vlagyimir Putyin sajtótitkára hétfőn.

stories.title: Nagy-Britannia nem vesz részt az iráni tengeri blokádban
stories.summary: A brit kormány célja nem a blokád támogatása, hanem a Hormuzi-szoros mielőbbi megnyitása

stories.title: Magyar Péter: Nem támogatjuk Ukrajna gyorsított EU-s csatlakozását – élőben a nemzetközi sajtótájékoztató
stories.summary: Kétharmados győzelem után kiáll a sajtó elé Magyar Péter, hogy a kormányváltásról és az előttünk álló időszakról beszéljen.

stories.title: Nagy Ervin szerint furcsa, hogy Orbán az irgalmatlan uszító kampány után teljesen európai módra gratulált Magyarnak
stories.summary: A Tisza Párt politikus magabiztosan nyert a választókerületében, gratulált is neki a Fidesz jelöltje, szerinte teljesen korrekt volt.

stories.title: „Én ennyire nem vagyok szent” – a mandineres Mészáros Nóra nem gratulál Magyar Péternek
stories.summary: „Inkább nagyon jó munkát kívánok!”, írta a Facebookon a műsorvezető.

stories.title: Ursula von der Leyen: Amint lehet, elkezdünk együtt dolgozni az új magyar kormánnyal a magyar emberek érdekében
stories.summary: A befagyasztott uniós forrásokra is utalt ezzel az Európai Bizottság elnöke, aki azt mondta, „kivételes este volt a tegnapi”, Európa ma magyar, ez nem kérdés.

stories.title: Előkerült Szijjártó Péter
stories.summary: A külügyminiszter vasárnap nem volt ott a Fidesz eredményváróján, nem posztolt a közösségi médiában. Hétfőn Budapesten fotózták le.

stories.title: Grafikonokon mutatjuk a Tisza elsöprő győzelmét
stories.summary: A korábbi választási eredményekkel összehasonlítva látszik csak igazán, mekkorát nyert Magyar Péter pártja.

stories.title: How Hungary's knife-edge election could impact the US and Russia
stories.summary: The BBC's Europe Editor Katya Adler reports from Prime Minister Victor Orbán's home town of Felscút.

stories.title: How much sway does Trump have over Hungarian voters?
stories.summary: US President Trump has backed Hungarian PM Orban. It's unclear whether the endorsement will help in Hungary's election — the European hard-right has struggled with Trump's recent anti-Europe policies and threats.

stories.title: Hungary election: Orban concedes 'painful' defeat to Magyar
stories.summary: Veteran Prime Minister Viktor Orban has acknowledged the landslide victory of Peter Magyar and his Tisza party in Hungary's parliamentary election. Magyar has promised sweeping reforms.

stories.title: Record turnout expected as parliamentary election in Hungary gets under way
stories.summary: Polls opened at 6am across the country, and will close 7pm local time. So far, all figures point to a record turnout.

stories.title: An environmental disaster in Moldova has Russia’s fingerprints all over it | Paula Erizanu
stories.summary: The Ukraine war on our doorstep is a constant threat. Contaminated drinking water is a dangerous new twist In the second week of March, the nature vlogger Ilie Cojocari went out to film the arrival of spring on the Nistru (Dniester) river, 70 metres away from his home in Naslavcea, a village bordering Ukraine on the northernmost point of Moldova. But as he approached the river he could smell the stench of oil rising up from the water and see dark spots floating on its surface. Something was wron

stories.title: As Hungary gears up for a historic election, DW answers a few key questions
stories.summary: Hungary's election on April 12 is the most important for the state since 1989, and also of historic significance for the EU. DW takes a more detailed look.

stories.title: Hungary Election: An Orbán victory would be good news for Europe
stories.summary: Hungary elections: An Orbán victory would be good news for Europe, writes Gerolf Annemans of the “Patriots for Europe” group in the European Parliament in an op-ed for Euronews. Annemans says Orbán has become a symbol of resistance to centralisation.

stories.title: Hungarians speak to the Guardian before decisive election – video
stories.summary: Voters will head to the polls on Sunday in what is seen as Europe’s most consequential election of the year. The Guardian's Jakub Krupa and Flora Garamvolgyi speak to Hungarians in Budapest before the vote Continue reading...

stories.title: Hungary: Scandal, fear and hope ahead of vote
stories.summary: Fake news and a growing number of political scandals: Hungary hasn't seen an election this fraught since after 1989's collapse of the communist dictatorship.

stories.title: Hungary Elections: There is hope that Peter Magyar can deliver a new beginning
stories.summary: What will Hungary’s elections bring? In an op-ed for Euronews, EU politician Valérie Hayer expresses hope that Péter Magyar could usher in a new political beginning, while warning that another term for Viktor Orbán risks cementing irreversible democratic backsliding.

stories.title: Even if Victor Orbán is ousted on Sunday, Hungary’s return to liberal democracy is not guaranteed | Gabriela Greilinger and Cas Mudde
stories.summary: Challenger Péter Magyar is no progressive – and after 16 years of creeping authoritarianism, the PM has embedded Fidesz in the Hungarian state On Sunday, Hungarians will go to the polls to decide on their country’s direction for the next four years in an election that looks as if it will be a nail-biter. Viktor Orbán, Europe’s longest-serving prime minister – who has been in power for 16 years and transformed his country into an electoral autocracy – could lose the election . Ahead of the vote,

stories.title: Fact check: Orban points to Ukraine over pipeline bomb
stories.summary: Orban strongly suggests explosives found near a key gas pipeline in Serbia are evidence of Ukrainian sabotage. Serbia's intelligence chief publicly disagrees.

stories.title: Hungary election: Voters want new EU approach, but tough Ukraine stance, poll shows
stories.summary: A new poll ahead of Hungary's election suggests that most voters want at least minor changes to ties with the EU. But it also found skepticism of financial support for Ukraine, and voters prioritizing domestic issues.

stories.title: JD Vance criticizes Zelenskyy and Ukraine in Hungary on visit campaigning for Viktor Orban
stories.summary: US Vice President JD Vance has criticized EU leaders and Ukraine's Volodymyr Zelenskyy during his visit to Hungary, echoing Prime Minister Viktor Orban's campaign messaging. The Kremlin chimed in from Moscow, too.

stories.title: Tourists to Australia would have social media accounts vetted under Trumpian Coalition plan
stories.summary: In hardline rhetoric, leader Angus Taylor also says ‘many’ prospective migrants would be a ‘net drain’ on the country Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast A Coalition government would end Australia’s non-discriminatory immigration program and introduce Trump-style social media vetting for visa applicants, as Angus Taylor accuses Labor of allowing migrants of “subversive intent” into the country. As the opposition loses support to Pauline Hanson’s One Nation, Taylor says t

stories.title: Five killed in strikes on alleged drug boats in eastern Pacific, US military says
stories.summary: Attacks on Saturday bring number of people killed in boat strikes by US military to at least 168 The US military said that it blew up two boats accused of smuggling drugs in the eastern Pacific Ocean, killing a total of five people and leaving one survivor, as the Trump administration pursues its campaign against alleged traffickers in Latin America while preparing a naval blockade of Iranian ports. The attacks on Saturday bring the number of people who have been killed in boat strikes by the US

stories.title: Britain won’t support Trump’s Iran blockade, says Starmer
stories.summary: LONDON — Britain is not supporting Donald Trump’s blockade of the Strait of Hormuz, Keir Starmer said Monday. The U.K. leader said in a BBC radio interview that he is focused on getting the vital shipping route open, as Britain and France prepare to co-host a summit on the issue this week. Starmer, who has come under fire from the U.S. president for not providing full military support for U.S. and Israeli strikes in Iran, said he is “very concerned” about the impact of the war on peo

stories.title: Labor ‘not considering’ joining US blockade of strait of Hormuz – as it happened
stories.summary: This blog is now closed Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Government to fund ads for helpline on pornography websites The child abuse prevention service is operated by Jesuit Social Services , which has run a trial scheme since 2022. It will now be able to expand its coverage as a result of commonwealth funding, and do so permanently. We know that it’s really important to work with people offending or at risk of offending, so we can intervene earlier, and ultimately ke

stories.title: ‘The perception is Carney is a wartime leader’: why Canada’s PM could secure a majority
stories.summary: As Trump’s actions spark a desire for stability, analysts say Carney is in effect assembling a union government Canada’s prime minister, Mark Carney, is on the brink of securing a majority government, with his Liberal party poised to win at least two closely watched byelections and courting an “almost unprecedented” string of defections from rival parties. Carney’s ability to turn a strong minority into a narrow majority through electoral gains and floor crossing has strengthened his reputation

stories.title: Iran war: US military to blockade Iranian ports
stories.summary: President Donald Trump wants to use the blockade to stop Iran from collecting transit fees and to cut off its oil revenue. Oil prices rose sharply after the announcement. Follow DW for more.

stories.title: Nigeria Air Force orders investigation after dozens killed in airstrike
stories.summary: There are reports that more than 100 people were killed, although the authorities have not commented.

stories.title: Trump says US will blockade strait of Hormuz after Iran peace talks fail
stories.summary: Iran warns move would breach ceasefire as US president also repeats threats to strike critical infrastructure Middle East crisis – live updates Donald Trump has said the US will begin blockading the strait of Hormuz in an attempt to take control of the strategic waterway from Iran in the aftermath of failed peace negotiations between the countries in Pakistan. The US president also threatened to bomb Iran’s water treatment facilities, power plants and bridges if Tehran did not agree to abandon i

stories.title: Pope Leo says Trump won’t silence him as US-Vatican feud erupts over Iran
stories.summary: Pope Leo said Monday that he was not scared of the White House, after U.S. President Donald Trump launched a stinging attack on the Catholic church leader. “I have no fear of the Trump administration,” Leo told reporters on a plane to Algeria after sparking Trump’s fury by criticizing the U.S.-Israeli bombing campaign in Iran. “I do believe in the message of the gospel, as a peacemaker,” Leo said, adding he will “not shy away from pronouncing [that] message.” The pontiff added, &#822

stories.title: Israeli strike kills paramedic, says Lebanese Red Cross – as it happened
stories.summary: This blog is now closed. Our live coverage continues here A post about an hour ago on the Israel Defense Forces Telegram channel claimed that overnight, the IDF “identified a rocket launcher positioned and ready to launch toward the State of Israel in the area of Jouaiyya in southern Lebanon”. Shortly after the identification, the launcher was struck and dismantled in a rapid closure cycle, thwarting the launch before it could be carried out. Continue reading...

stories.title: Barnaby Joyce says criminals need ‘redemption’ after Pauline Hanson sacks convicted rapist
stories.summary: Liberal senator James Paterson had called decision by One Nation to rehire Sean Black ‘absolutely extraordinary’ Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Pauline Hanson says she has sacked the convicted rapist who worked as One Nation’s campaign manager, but accused Coalition MPs of playing “gutter politics” by criticising her decision to rehire him. Sean Black, who in 2018 was jailed for rape and subsequently lost his ap

stories.title: Keir Starmer defends plan for closer alignment with EU rules
stories.summary: PM rejects claim plan is integration with EU ‘by stealth’, saying changes will happen only if parliament passes law UK politics live – latest updates Europe live – latest updates Business live – latest updates Keir Starmer has defended plans for the UK to align more closely with some EU rules without parliamentary votes, saying a closer relationship with Europe “is in the UK’s best interest”, particularly given the international turmoil over the Iran war. Speaking to the BBC after the Guardian r

stories.title: Trump calls Pope Leo ‘weak’ and ‘terrible’ in extraordinary broadside
stories.summary: President says US-born pope is ‘not doing a very good job’ in unprecedented criticism of leader of Catholic church Middle East crisis – live updates Europe live – latest updates Donald Trump has delivered an extraordinary broadside against Pope Leo XIV, saying he does not think the US-born leader of the Catholic church is “doing a very good job” and that he is “a very liberal person”, while also suggesting the pontiff should “stop catering to the radical left”. In response, Leo, who arrived in A

stories.summary: President contrasts his health with challenger Flávio Bolsonaro, who fainted during a TV debate The Brazilian president, Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva, is seeking to lunge and leg press his way to a historic fourth term, as the octogenarian politician uses a flurry of workout videos to convince voters he is fighting fit ahead of October’s crunch election. Lula looks set to face off against a senator almost half his age in what will be the leftist’s seventh presidential campaign since he first sought

stories.title: Merck's Keytruda: A lifesaving drug, a global divide

stories.title: Germany cuts fuel tax as oil prices surge on Iran blockade

stories.title: Political turmoil in Indian border state as nine million lose voting rights
stories.summary: Nine million voters have been dropped from rolls in West Bengal, raising concerns over exclusion and fairness.

stories.title: EU hires former von der Leyen aide as top competition official
stories.summary: BRUSSELS — Irishman Anthony Whelan was named on Monday as director-general of the EU’s competition department, installing the former digital adviser to European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen to the pivotal role after a lengthy search. Competition Commissioner Teresa Ribera announced the appointment in a social media post, citing Whelan’s “deep experience in European policymaking.” Whelan takes the helm at DG COMP at a time of increased scrutiny of Big Tech and as a review of the EU’s

stories.title: €1m Picasso painting to be won for €100 in charity raffle
stories.summary: Number of tickets to win Tête de Femme will be capped at 120,000 and proceeds will go to Alzheimer’s research A raffle in France is offering the chance to win a portrait by Pablo Picasso for the price of a €100 (£87) ticket, with proceeds going to Alzheimer’s research. Picasso painted the gouache-on-paper Tête de Femme (Head of a Woman) in 1941. The raffle organisers’ online sales platform says the number of tickets will be capped at 120,000, meaning the draw could net €12m if they are all sold.

stories.title: Weather tracker: Super Typhoon Sinlaku threatens Mariana Islands
stories.summary: Rapidly strengthening storm brings destructive winds, flooding risk and dangerous seas to western Pacific The Mariana Islands archipelago in the western Pacific, home to the US territories of Guam and the Northern Mariana Islands, are bracing for extreme weather early this week as Super Typhoon Sinlaku approaches. The system originated as a cluster of thunderstorms over the seas of Micronesia before strengthening into a tropical storm and then a typhoon on Friday and Saturday. Continue reading..

stories.title: And the election winner is … the candidate who can afford Africa’s soaring nomination fees
stories.summary: Presidential elections in Djibouti and Benin at the weekend highlighted how a costly electoral system is reshaping democracy Alexis Mohamed would have loved to stand against his former boss. A longtime adviser to Djibouti’s president, Ismail Omar Guelleh, Mohamed resigned last September, citing democratic regression in the country. But at the election at the weekend, Mohamed was not on the ballot. Now outside the country, he says he cannot return home to file nomination papers or campaign freely

stories.title: Kratom poisonings soar in US as experts blame synthetic versions and caution against bans
stories.summary: Experts say natural kratom may offer benefits and blame synthetic derivatives for surge in poisonings noted by CDC A recent Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) report shows that kratom poisonings have soared in the US, but experts say this is probably due to synthetic derivatives like 7-OH, and that blanket kratom bans could harm people using natural kratom to aid pain management or addiction recovery. stories.title: Hate small talk? You may enjoy that ‘dull’ chat more than you think, say researchers
stories.summary: Participants reported enjoying the human connection regardless of whether they thought the topic was dull The human aversion to dull experiences was nailed by the author Paulo Coelho when he declared: “I can stand defeats, pain, anger. But I can’t stand boredom.” But the natural desire to avoid boring conversations comes at a cost, according to researchers, who found that people enjoyed chatting about tedious topics far more than they expected. Continue reading...

stories.title: Nigerian airstrike targeting jihadists reportedly kills at least 100 civilians
stories.summary: Officials confirm misfire as Amnesty gives death toll after speaking to survivors of strike on market in Yobe state A Nigerian air force strike targeting jihadist rebels hit a market in north-east Nigeria, killing more than 100 people and injuring many others, Amnesty International and local media have said. Officials confirmed a misfire had occurred but did not provide details. Continue reading...

stories.title: Why Africa remains divided over Macky Sall's UN bid
stories.summary: Burundi nominated former Senegal president Macky Sall for UN chief, but Africa's backing fractured. AU procedural disputes, objections and regional politics scuppered a united endorsement.

stories.title: Pope Leo opens first African visit with stop in Algeria
stories.summary: Africa is the fastest-growing region within the Catholic Church. The pontiff is launching his first African tour with a visit to Algeria, a majority Muslim country.

stories.title: World's oldest gorilla celebrates 69th birthday at Berlin Zoo

stories.title: Peru general elections: Fujimori leading, runoff likely
stories.summary: Keiko Fujimori looked set to face a conservative rival in a runoff vote. More than 30 candidates had been contention for the presidency.

stories.title: Lufthansa pilots launch 2-day strike, the fourth this year; hundreds of flights canceled
stories.summary: Pilots who are members of the Vereinigung Cockpit trade union are striking on Monday and Tuesday. It's the fourth strike to hit Lufthansa and some of its subsidiaries this year, just days after cabin crew downed tools.

stories.title: Pope Leo: I am not afraid of Trump administration

stories.title: Germany: 1 in 4 residents has 'immigration history'
stories.summary: Every fourth person living in Germany has either moved there from abroad or been born to parents who are both immigrants. The new statistics are a modest increase on the 2024 figures.

stories.title: Australia appoints first female army chief
stories.summary: Susan Coyle is set to take over as Australia's army chief in July, becoming the first woman to hold that position in the country's history. Coyle joined the army at age 17.

stories.title: 'Peacemaker Pakistan': Reality or facade?

stories.title: Hacking women's political underrepresentation, Dutch-style
stories.summary: Some 504 women were elected in recent Dutch local elections, with a "Vote for a Woman" campaign boosting their chances to gather preferential votes. Despite the record figure, women remain underrepresented.

stories.title: Zelenskyy, Putin mark Easter amid truce breach allegations

stories.title: Nigeria: Many dead after military bombs village market

stories.title: Eight to be awarded for bravery in Bondi Junction stabbing

stories.summary: Surveys show Friedrich Merz as the most unpopular leader across 24 democracies, far behind Narendra Modi, Lee Jae-myung and Andrej Babiš in approval.

stories.title: Carney on verge of Liberal majority government as votes cast in three by-elections
stories.summary: One more seat could solidify the Canadian PM's hold on power after several Conservatives defected.

stories.title: Britney Spears goes into rehab after driving under the influence arrest

stories.summary: Asha Bhosle died in Mumbai on Sunday after being admitted to the hospital following a heart attack.

stories.title: Cherry Blossom Festival in Berlin attracts many tourists this spring
stories.summary: Each spring, Japanese cherry blossoms across Berlin attract many visitors to the German capital. These vibrant pink and white trees, which bloom for only a few days, can be found scattered throughout parks across the city.

stories.title: Leo becomes first Pope to visit Algeria at start of major Africa tour
stories.summary: Leo XIV wants the world's attention on a continent vital to the Church's future, an aide says.

Darauf einigten sich die Spitzen von Union und SPD in ihrem Beratungsmarathon über das Wochenende. Das geht aus dem Beschlussdokument hervor, das POLITICO vorliegt ( hier ). stories.title: Benin election: Finance minister Wadagni expected to win
stories.summary: Finance Minister Romuald Wadagni is the favourite to win after being endorsed by outgoing president Patrice Talon. Wadagni's only rival, Paul Hounkpe, has a much lower profile.

stories.title: US-Iran talks: What prevented a deal and what's next?

stories.summary: U.S. President Donald Trump ordered a naval blockade of the Strait of Hormuz after talks with Iran in Islamabad ended without a deal. The move ramps up pressure as both sides blame each other, while Pakistan urges restraint and the ceasefire hangs in the balance.

stories.title: Pope Leo XIV urges immediate ceasefire as Israel-Hezbollah clashes rage
stories.summary: Pope Leo XIV called for an immediate ceasefire on Sunday as fighting between Israel and Hezbollah continues, urging all parties to respect international law and protect civilians.

stories.title: Whale Timmy: Can whale songs still save him? Biologist prepares autopsy
stories.summary: For days now, marine biologists have been assuming that there is no rescue for Timmy the humpback whale stranded in the Bay of Wismar. The Environment Minister of Mecklenburg-Western Pomerania is now talking about a "minimally invasive attempt to mobilise the whale".

stories.title: No clouds on the horizon: France and Portugal strengthen their relationship with Treaty of Porto
stories.summary: The Treaty of Friendship and Cooperation between the two countries entered into force this Sunday, a partnership 'at the service of the French and the Portuguese, and of a Europe that decides for itself'.

stories.summary: Ha olyan jó az Artemis–2, miért nincs Artemis–3? Nyugi, lesz.

stories.title: Itt a szezon!
A Tisza Párt megnyerte a választást, de mi történik az új kormány megalakulásáig?
stories.summary: A köztársasági elnöknek május 12-ig kell összehívnia az új parlament alakuló ülését, ahol javaslatot tesz a miniszterelnök személyére. Addig a kormány mozgástere nincs korlátozva, és a fideszes parlament még bármit megszavazhat. Csakhogy a tiszás kétharmad aztán visszavonhatja ezeket.

stories.title: XIV. Leó pápa válaszolt Trump beszólásaira
stories.summary: Az amerikai elnök szörnyűnek nevezte, majd azt mondta róla, hogy azért választották meg, mert amerikai.

stories.title: Pintér Bence: Ha lesz vagyonvisszaszerzési hivatal, szerintem tudunk együtt dolgozni
stories.summary: Győr ellenzéki polgármestere az eredményhirdetés előtt sorsdöntőnek nevezte a választás kimenetelét. Az eredmények ismeretében ismét nekifut a közgyűlés feloszlatásának.

stories.title: Ketten meghaltak az Ukrajna elleni, a húsvéti tűzszünet ellenére végrehajtott orosz támadásokban
stories.summary: Az ukrán vezérkar szerint az orosz haderő 32 óra alatt 10,721 alkalommal sértette meg a tűzszünetet.

stories.title: 30 millió forintos óvadékot ajánlott fel a Váci úti felüljárónál halálos balesetet okozó sofőr, a bíróság nem fogadta el
stories.summary: A bíróság egy hónapra elrendelte a török állampolgárságú férfi letartóztatását.

stories.title: Peruvians go to polls hoping to break cycle of instability
stories.summary: Crime and corruption top voter concerns in highly unpredictable election with 35 candidates for president Peruvians go to the polls on Sunday hoping to break a cycle of instability that has produced nine presidents in a decade as well as surging violent crime , corruption scandals and overwhelming distrust in institutions and politicians. About 27 million people who are eligible to vote must choose between a record 35 presidential candidates as well as contenders for the bicameral congress – all

stories.title: UFOs: 'Most are reported by people out for an evening smoke'
stories.summary: Hansjürgen Köhler has been a volunteer UFO hunter for more than 50 years. He sees his work as scientific investigation, but sometimes also as caring for others.

stories.title: Germany: Betting on elections is illegal — but possible
stories.summary: Online gambling is gaining traction in Germany. Using prediction markets like Polymarket to bet on political events remains illegal, but it is almost impossible to police.

stories.title: Why this disillusioned Trump voter spends hours searching Epstein files
stories.summary: Many who support the president have expressed frustration with how Trump's former attorney general Pam Bondi handled the Epstein files.

stories.title: Ukraine : Russian drone attacks hit Sumy and Odesa as Easter truce looms
stories.summary: Russian drone strikes hit Sumy and Odesa, injuring two and damaging homes and energy sites, as Putin proposes a brief Easter ceasefire.

stories.title: US man in Bahamian jail after wife disappears into Atlantic waters during boat trip
stories.summary: Lynette and Brian Hooker, from Michigan, were years into a sailing adventure when Brian said his wife fell overboard Lynette Hooker bounced around the deck of the docked Soul Mate, smiled into the camera and proclaimed, “We’re finally leaving Kemah,” referring to a Texas port town. “It’s only been four months,” she said as her husband, Brian, tugged on some rigging as they got ready to set sail. Continue reading...

stories.title: A festival of young European photography
stories.summary: The Circulation(s) photo festival has returned to Paris for its 16th edition. On show are works by 26 young European photographers. The projects capture the pulse of young European photography, its intuitions, challenges and commitments Circulation(s) will take place at the Centquatre-Paris from 21 March to 17 May 2026 Continue reading...

stories.title: Floods, power outages and hundreds evacuated as Cyclone Vaianu lashes New Zealand’s North Island
stories.summary: Cyclone crossed coast near Maketu peninsula, packing destructive winds exceeding 130km/h (80 mph), heavy rain and large swells Cyclone Vaianu made landfall in New Zealand’s North Island on Sunday, triggering floods, power outages and forcing hundreds to evacuate . The cyclone crossed the coast near the Maketu peninsula, packing destructive winds exceeding 130km/h (80 mph), heavy rain and large swells, national weather provider MetService said, describing Vaianu as a “life-threatening” system. Co

stories.title: JD Vance says talks failed due to Iran’s refusal to give up nuclear programme
stories.summary: Iranian delegates in Islamabad say Washington needs to do more to win their trust if talks to resolve US-Iran conflict are to be successful Middle East crisis – live updates The US vice-president, JD Vance, has blamed the failure of marathon negotiations with Iran on the country’s refusal to abandon its nuclear weapons programme, while Iranian delegates have claimed Washington needs to do more to win their trust. Vance, who left Islamabad on Sunday morning after 21 hours of talks with Iranian of

stories.title: At least 30 killed in crush at historic fortress in Haiti
stories.summary: Officials said many killed at popular tourist site were young, with more people reported injured or missing At least 30 people, many of them young, have died and dozens more are reported to have been injured after a crush at a mountaintop fortress in northern Haiti that is a popular tourist spot. Jean Henri Petit, the head of civil protection for the country’s Nord department, said the incident took place on Saturday at Citadelle Henry, also known as Citadelle Laferrière, a large 19th-century fo

stories.title: Is this Syrian doctor one of the 80% Germany wants to remove?
stories.summary: Chancellor Friedrich Merz has ignited a debate by declaring that 80% of Syrians would be repatriated. Does this mean that Germany also intends to deport those who are integrated and whom the country needs?

stories.title: Ukrainian civilians abducted, tortured in Russian prisons
stories.summary: Russia has been holding thousands of Ukrainian civilians behind bars for years, in breach of international humanitarian law. Families have been fighting for their loved ones to be released, some for more than a decade.

stories.title: Russian Memorial human rights NGO labeled 'extremist'
stories.summary: Russia's Supreme Court has labeled the human rights organization Memorial as "extremist." Experts say this represents a new level of repression in Russia.

stories.title: Ireland clears refinery blockade as fuel protests trigger nationwide shortages
stories.summary: Authorities used force to reopen the country's only refinery, after days of disruption over petrol prices left fuel pumps dry and threatened emergency services operations.

stories.title: Is Iran unable to locate its mines in the Strait of Hormuz?
stories.summary: The New York Times reports, citing officials, that Iran may not have accurate records of the locations of all the mines it has laid. Reports suggest that the planting was carried out in a random and disorganised.

stories.title: Ukraine and Russia exchange prisoners ahead of Orthodox Easter ceasefire
stories.summary: The warring sides exchanged 175 prisoners of war each in a mediation facilitated by the United Arab Emirates, officials from both nations said.

stories.title: Armenia and Azerbaijan civil societies hold new round of broad talks to advance peace process
stories.summary: Armenian and Azerbaijan’s representatives met for the fourth round of talks as part of the so-called Peace Bridge Initiative to advance the historic peace process between the two South Caucasus neighbours.

stories.title: 'It's a special thing to be on Planet Earth': Artemis crew welcomed home in Houston
stories.summary: The four astronauts flew around the Moon in a nine-day voyage that took them further from Earth than any humans in history.

stories.title: We spoke to the man making viral Lego-style AI videos for Iran. Experts say it's powerful propaganda
stories.summary: "Slopaganda" is too weak a term to capture how powerful this "highly sophisticated" content is, one expert says.

stories.title: Trump threatens US 'blockade' of Strait of Hormuz after Iran ceasefire talks in Pakistan fail
stories.summary: Trump said in a social media post he has “instructed our Navy to seek and interdict every vessel in International Waters that has paid a toll to Iran. No one who pays an illegal toll will have safe passage on the high seas”.

stories.title: At least 30 feared dead in crush at Haitian tourist site
stories.summary: The incident occurred at a "tourist event attended by many young people", Haiti's prime minister says.

stories.title: 'We need real peace': Ukrainians have little faith in Easter truce
stories.summary: Easter festivities are muted in Kharkiv as Ukrainians expect fighting to flare up again after a weekend truce.

stories.title: UK starts crackdown on EU citizens’ post-Brexit rights
stories.summary: Concerns raised over use of travel data in determining if people are ‘continuously’ in Britain after HMRC fiasco UK ministers are to start removing post-Brexit residency rights from EU citizens who are no longer “continuously” living in the country. The initiative is legal under the 2020 Brexit withdrawal agreement, but the decision to use travel data to partly determine absences has raised concerns after the HMRC fiasco in which almost 20,000 parents were stripped of child benefits because of i

stories.title: The week around the world in 20 pictures
stories.summary: Crisis in the Middle East, Russian shelling in Ukraine, Artemis’s lunar flyby and World Press Photo winners – the past seven days as captured by the world’s leading photojournalists Warning: this gallery contains images some readers may find distressing Continue reading...

stories.title: New Zealand’s North Island braces for Cyclone Vaianu with thousands ordered to evacuate
stories.summary: Vaianu, forecast to bring heavy rain and winds of up to 130 kmh (80 mph), is expected to hit on Sunday Thousands of New Zealanders were ordered to evacuate their homes on Saturday as the country’s North Island braced for Cyclone Vaianu, which authorities warned could cause coastal flooding and landslides. Vaianu, forecast to bring heavy rain and winds of up to 130 km/h (80 mp/h), was expected to hit on Sunday, then pass west of the remote Chatham Islands on Monday, the country’s weather forecast

stories.title: Albanese didn’t return with shiploads of diesel. That doesn’t mean his Singapore visit wasn’t a success
stories.summary: Having received assurances from Singapore over refined fuels, diesel supply will surely be next on the prime minister’s agenda Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Anthony Albanese isn’t coming back from Singapore with a shipload of diesel in his checked baggage. That doesn’t mean his whistle-stop visit wasn’t a success, or that it won’t be seen in future as a pivotal moment if fuel stocks continue to be choked by the ongoing conflict in the Middle East. The government ne

stories.title: First gay rights movement: Berlin's wild 1920s queer history
stories.summary: Before the Nazis took power, Berlin was a center of LGBTQ+ rights and queer culture, with research institutes, a flourishing nightlife and one of the earliest gay rights' movements.

stories.title: Ongoing wars 'not failure of UN but of member states', General Assembly President tells Euronews
stories.summary: The head of the United Nations' main policy-making organ responded to mounting criticism of her organisation in a Euronews interview, arguing the abuse of vetoes by key members is blocking progress.

stories.title: Former hostage In Iran faces surreal tax troubles in France after release
stories.summary: Former French hostage in Iran, Benjamin Brière, said his release did not end his ordeal, as he struggled with taxes, benefits and reintegration back home.
